payload.subject: Work order: B3 — quantile-binned recalibration across all 10 dimensions

background.problem: calibrate.py uses raw isotonic regression. TE has plateaus spanning 1.51 raw units (15% of scale, CRITICAL). Other dimensions have smaller but measurable plateaus. B2 (HI dead zone) demonstrated that quantile-binned pre-aggregation (n_bins=20) resolves this class of problem — HI MAE improved 3.9%, dead zone at [6.0045, 7.2539] differentiated. B3 applies the same fix to all 10 dimensions.
background.prior_art: B2 fix (Session 26, psq-agent commit 9629412): quantile-binned isotonic regression with n_bins=20 on HI dimension. isotonic-v2-2026-03-06 deployed. Methodology proven. B3 extends to remaining 9 dimensions.
background.data_preservation: ~24,289 texts scored under current calibration. Observatory carries 713+ stories with PSQ scores. DI Phase A Pass 1 used v23-calibrated scores (27 stories). Transport messages reference specific PSQ values. All historical data must remain usable.

specification.rationale: B3 changes only the post-hoc calibration layer, not the model. Raw scores (DistilBERT inference output) remain identical regardless of calibration function. Full corpus rescore (approach B) solves a problem that does not exist — the raw scores have not changed. Approach C provides consistent scores across the entire corpus without touching inference.

steps.action: Apply quantile-binned pre-aggregation (n_bins=20) to all 10 dimensions in calibrate.py
steps.detail: Same methodology as B2 HI fix. Fit new isotonic models per dimension using the quantile-binned approach. Produce new calibration.json (v3).

steps.action: Dead-zone scan on all 10 recalibrated dimensions
steps.detail: Verify no plateau exceeds 0.5 raw units after recalibration. Report per-dimension: largest plateau width (raw units), MAE before/after, calibration range.

steps.action: Archive old calibration models as paired artifacts
steps.detail: Save both old (isotonic-v2) and new (isotonic-v3) calibration models in a format that allows deterministic re-mapping. Both models map raw_score → calibrated_score; the conversion function chains: old_calibrated → raw (inverse of old model) → new_calibrated (forward through new model).

Write a function that takes (raw_score, dimension) and returns new_calibrated_score. Since v3 responses already carry raw_score, any consumer can re-map historical scores through the new calibration without re-running inference. The function should also accept old_calibrated_score and invert through the old model when raw_score is unavailable.

Replace calibration.json on Hetzner. Update calibration_version string in server.js (addresses the hardcoded version string flagged in turn 15 epistemic flags). Verify /health returns new version. Verify /score returns recalibrated values.

Send transport message to unratified-agent with: new calibration version, conversion function availability, recommendation to re-map cached PSQ scores using raw_score field. No breaking API changes — v3/v3.1 schema unchanged.

scope_boundaries: DO: recalibrate all 10 dimensions with quantile-binned isotonic (n_bins=20)
DO: provide conversion function for historical score re-mapping
DO: archive old calibration models for reproducibility
DO NOT: rescore the full corpus (unnecessary — raw scores unchanged)
DO NOT: retrain the model (separate work item, blocked on concordance study)
DO NOT: change the v3/v3.1 response schema

success_criteria: No dimension has a calibration plateau exceeding 0.5 raw units
Per-dimension MAE improves or holds (no regression)
Old and new calibration models archived as paired artifacts

relationship_to_other_work.concordance_study: B3 does NOT depend on the cross-scorer concordance study (turn 16 gate). B3 changes calibration, not model weights or training data. The concordance gate blocks further LLM-scored batches entering training — B3 operates entirely in the post-inference calibration layer.
relationship_to_other_work.future_retrain: When the retrain happens (post-concordance), it should use v3-calibrated values as training targets. B3 should complete BEFORE retrain so the retrain benefits from improved calibration baseline. This ordering was already noted in TODO.md.
relationship_to_other_work.di_phase_a: DI Phase A Pass 1 used v23-calibrated PSQ scores (27 stories). The conversion function allows re-mapping those scores to v3-calibrated values if needed. Pass 2 will use whatever calibration is live at scoring time.

claims.text: B3 changes only the calibration layer, not the model. Raw scores remain identical. Full corpus rescore is unnecessary.
claims.confidence_basis: Calibration is a post-hoc monotonic mapping applied after inference. The DistilBERT model produces raw_score; calibrate.py maps it to calibrated_score. Changing the mapping does not affect the model output.

claims.text: The B2 fix (HI quantile-binned isotonic, n_bins=20) provides proven methodology for B3.
claims.confidence_basis: B2 deployed (psq-agent commit 9629412), MAE improved 3.9%, dead zone differentiated. Same approach, extended to remaining 9 dimensions.

action_gate.gate_condition: none — B3 is independent of the concordance study gate (turn 16). B3 operates in the calibration layer, not the training layer.

epistemic_flags: The inverse mapping (old_calibrated → raw) requires the old isotonic model to be invertible. Isotonic regression is piecewise constant — the inverse is a set-valued mapping (multiple raw values map to the same calibrated value). The conversion function should use the raw_score field (available in v3 responses) as the primary input, with old_calibrated inversion as a fallback for cases where raw_score is unavailable.

n_bins=20 was validated for HI only. Other dimensions may have different optimal bin counts depending on their raw score distributions. The work order specifies n_bins=20 as default but psq-agent should evaluate per-dimension and adjust if a dimension shows poor results.
